analysis.executive_summary: Audit of semgrep.dev/docs covered 1,116 pages with 100% crawl coverage and 91.5% overall confidence. Documentation contains 11 confirmed broken internal links requiring immediate remediation. API reference coverage stands at 72.73% with 311 API pages detected. SEO/geo issue rate of 16.53% indicates significant discoverability problems. Example reliability estimate of 86.21% and last-updated coverage of 66.22% suggest moderate maintenance gaps.
strengths: Complete crawl coverage (100%) across 1,116 pages with high crawl confidence (95%)
Strong API documentation presence with 311 API pages detected and coverage determination successful
High overall audit confidence at 91.5% with robust link verification (95% confidence)
Example reliability estimate of 86.21% shows majority of code samples are likely functional
Multi-project topology successfully mapped with 1,116 discovered pages in scope
risks: 11 confirmed broken internal links degrading user navigation and trust in documentation quality
SEO/geo issue rate of 16.53% (approximately 185 pages) limiting search discoverability and international reach
API reference coverage gap of 27.27% leaves significant portions of API undocumented
Only 66.22% of pages show last-updated metadata, obscuring content freshness for 377+ pages
Example reliability at 86.21% means approximately 154 pages may contain broken or outdated code samples
817 trap URLs skipped during crawl may indicate URL structure issues or infinite pagination patterns
13.79% example unreliability could erode developer trust and increase support burden

prioritized_actions.action: Fix all 11 confirmed broken internal links to restore navigation integrity
Audit and remediate SEO/geo issues on 185 affected pages to improve discoverability
Expand API reference documentation to close 27.27% coverage gap (target 90%+)
Implement last-updated metadata on 377 pages missing freshness indicators
Validate and update code examples on 154 pages with reliability concerns

limitations: External audit cannot verify actual functionality of code examples, only structural patterns and syntax validity
API coverage detection relies on page structure heuristics and may miss non-standard API documentation formats
Freshness assessment depends on explicit last-updated metadata; actual content currency cannot be independently verified
SEO/geo issue detection covers technical factors only; content quality, keyword optimization, and competitive positioning require separate analysis
Broken link verification performed at audit time; links may break or be fixed after report generation
